message:
feat(raptorq/gf256): add SIMD kernel dispatch infrastructure with AVX2/NEON scaffolds

Introduce a runtime-detected kernel dispatch layer for bulk GF(256)
operations (add_slice, mul_slice, addmul_slice) to enable future
ISA-specific SIMD optimizations without changing caller code.

Key changes:
- Add Gf256Kernel enum (Scalar, X86Avx2, Aarch64Neon) with OnceLock-based
  detection via std::is_x86_feature_detected / is_aarch64_feature_detected
- Implement NibbleTables (Halevi-Shacham nibble-decomposed multiplication)
  using std::simd portable SIMD for 16-byte-at-a-time GF(256) multiply
- Import platform intrinsics (core::arch::x86_64, core::arch::aarch64) for
  AVX2 and NEON instruction sets — dispatch scaffolds currently delegate to
  scalar core to preserve correctness while establishing the stable ABI
- Add mul_nibble_tables() helper for pre-computing low/high nibble lookup
  tables from the existing compile-time MUL_TABLES
- Enable #![feature(portable_simd)] in lib.rs crate root
- Public active_kernel() API exposes the selected dispatch family

The dispatch scaffolds (x86_avx2, aarch64_neon) intentionally reuse the
scalar implementation today; the contract is stable so future commits can
drop in optimized intrinsic paths without touching callers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
